scientific_contract.question: Does the compact cold-start record handoff candidate improve external canonical PRD indexing versus current 10x?
scientific_contract.hypothesis: The compact candidate should preserve external PRD authority, behavior, limits, and next action with less record spread than first-batch broad candidates.
scientific_contract.expected_behavior: Subject creates durable 10x records from the approved Nimbus Retention Controls Google Doc PRD without implementation edits.
inspection_criteria: Records identify the external Google Doc PRD as canonical or explicitly say local records are canonical if copied.
Records preserve status, revision, owner, URL/document ID, behavior scope, exclusions, acceptance scenarios, rollout/open-question status, limits, and next action.
Records avoid implementation edits, placeholder spread, missing source/provenance, and invented implementation names.
Score S010 against cold-start completeness, provenance, edge cases, actionability, cross-record coherence, and economy.
scientific_contract.quality_floor: Any wrong authority, implementation edit, missing PRD provenance, or future need to rediscover the PRD status and scope caps the candidate.

- [2026-06-28-nimbus-retention-controls-planning.md](/mnt/c/Users/butle/AppData/Local/Temp/10x-autoresearch-jetevw1v/workspace/.10x/tickets/2026-06-28-nimbus-retention-controls-planning.md) is a blocked planning ticket requiring the canonical Doc URL/document ID/export before implementation specs or executable tickets are derived.

No code, tests, migrations, or runtime artifacts were created.

# Nimbus Retention Controls Implementation Planning

## Scope

Create implementation-ready 10x specifications and executable tickets from the approved canonical Google Doc PRD titled "Nimbus Retention Controls."

## Blockers

- The canonical Google Doc URL, document ID, or approved export is not available in this workspace.
- The PRD body has not been read, so implementation semantics and acceptance criteria are unknown locally.

## Acceptance Criteria

- The canonical PRD is identified by URL, document ID, or approved export path.
- The PRD is read and cited in local 10x planning records.
- Independent behavioral surfaces are split into focused active specifications when required by 10x spec-first rules.
- Executable implementation tickets reference the focused governing specifications and include acceptance criteria, exclusions, evidence expectations, blockers, and dependencies.
- No implementation ticket contains unratified retention, permission, deletion, notification, audit, rollout, or operational semantics.

## Explicit Exclusions

- No code implementation.
- No tests, migrations, generated artifacts, or runtime changes.
- No inferred requirements from product name or common retention-control patterns.

## References

- .10x/specs/nimbus-retention-controls-prd.md

## Evidence Expectations

Planning evidence should include the PRD source identifier, access method, export or reading date, and a requirement-to-spec/ticket mapping sufficient for a future cold-start implementation agent.

## Progress and Notes

- 2026-06-28: User stated the approved Nimbus Retention Controls Google Doc PRD is canonical for implementation planning and requested durable 10x records without code implementation.
- 2026-06-28: Workspace inspection found no existing `.10x` records and no exposed Google Docs reader. Gmail searches for exact and related PRD terms returned no matching messages.

Status: active

Updated: 2026-06-28

# Nimbus Retention Controls PRD

## Purpose and Scope

This record indexes the approved external Google Doc PRD titled "Nimbus Retention Controls" for implementation planning.

The Google Doc PRD is canonical. This local 10x record is not a behavioral substitute for the PRD and does not restate requirements because the PRD body and URL were not available in this workspace on 2026-06-28.

## Canonical Source

- Source system: Google Docs
- Canonical artifact: approved Google Doc PRD titled "Nimbus Retention Controls"
- Approval status: approved, per user instruction on 2026-06-28
- Canonical URL / document ID: not provided in this workspace
- Local export path: none
- Local record role: thin index only; the external Google Doc remains canonical

## Implementation Authority

Future implementation planning MUST read the approved Google Doc PRD before creating implementation tickets, specifications derived from the PRD, tests, migrations, UI/API behavior, data retention rules, permissions, lifecycle behavior, notifications, operational procedures, or code.

Future agents MUST NOT infer retention semantics from the title "Nimbus Retention Controls" or from common retention-control patterns. The following are explicitly unratified in local 10x records until read from the canonical PRD or confirmed by the user:

- data classes and records affected by retention controls
- retention periods, legal holds, deletion, anonymization, archival, and recovery behavior
- user roles, permissions, approvals, and audit requirements
- UI, API, CLI, background job, or integration surfaces
- customer communication, notification, escalation, and operational ownership
- migration, rollout, backfill, monitoring, and failure-handling requirements
- acceptance criteria and verification procedures

## Required Planning Path

1. Obtain the canonical Google Doc URL, document ID, or an approved export of the PRD.
2. Read the PRD and classify each implementation-relevant requirement as PRD-backed, user-ratified, source-observed, or blocked.
3. Create focused 10x specifications for independent behavioral surfaces before executable implementation tickets.
4. Create implementation tickets only after the governing specifications are active and blockers are resolved.

## Explicit Exclusions

This record does not authorize implementation. It does not authorize deriving tests or behavior from assumptions. It does not supersede the external Google Doc PRD.

instruction_delivery.description: Arm instructions are supplied with Codex developer_instructions; the scenario prompt is passed separately.

control_isolation.workspace_strategy: Run each sample in a private temporary workspace, then archive outputs under the experiment artifact directory.
control_isolation.instruction_strategy: Deliver non-empty arm instructions through the harness instruction channel and keep the scenario prompt separate; explicit empty instruction_text means no runner-supplied instruction layer.
control_isolation.record_graph_strategy: Remove inherited .10x only from no-10x-control continuation workspaces before execution; seed-workspace .10x records remain available when they are the task surface.
control_isolation.limitation: Codex built-in system context and authenticated home remain outside this runner's control; this runner appends arm instructions through developer_instructions when non-empty.

harness_metadata.promotion_limit: Live subject output is candidate-quality evidence, not promotion authority.
